records.ety: [L. posse to be able, to have power + LL. comitatus a county, from comes, comitis, a count. See County, and Power.]

defs: The power of the county, or the citizens who may be summoned by the sheriff to assist the authorities in suppressing a riot, or executing any legal precept which is forcibly opposed.
A collection of people; a throng; a rabble.
quotes: As if the passion that rules were the sheriff of the place, and came off with all the posse.
notes: ☞ The word comitatus is often omitted, and posse alone used. “A whole posse of enthusiasts.”
